label: Create Order
description: The primary checkout operation. Validates the order, coordinates stock and payment, persists the order, and emits the order.created event.

business.summary: Accepts a customer's order request, validates it against business rules and available stock, initiates a Stripe payment intent, and records the order as awaiting payment. The customer receives an order ID to complete payment on the client side.
rules: An order must contain at least one line item with a positive quantity.
Every line item must be in stock at the requested quantity at the moment of order creation.
Orders with a total value over $10,000 are automatically flagged for manual review — they proceed to AWAITING_PAYMENT but are not auto-confirmed.
A customer may not place more than 5 orders within any rolling 24-hour window.
A Stripe PaymentIntent must be created before the order is persisted — an order without a payment intent is invalid.
If the PaymentIntent creation fails, no order record is created.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT
Request body passes schema validation (non-empty lineItems, valid quantities, shipping address present)
outcomes: Order is persisted with AWAITING_PAYMENT status
Customer receives an order ID and Stripe client_secret to complete payment on the client side
order.created event is emitted to Kafka
Order is flagged for manual review if total exceeds $10,000

failure_modes.condition: One or more line items are out of stock
failure_modes.response: Order is rejected with 409 Conflict. Customer is shown which items are unavailable and their current stock levels.

failure_modes.condition: Customer has exceeded the 5-orders-per-24h rate limit
failure_modes.response: Order is rejected with 429 Too Many Requests. Customer is told when they can place their next order via Retry-After header.
Order is rejected with 503. No order record is created. Customer is shown a payment system unavailable message.

failure_modes.condition: Database write fails after PaymentIntent is created
failure_modes.response: Order is rejected with 500. The Stripe PaymentIntent is voided asynchronously to avoid orphaned payment intents.

middleware_chain.name: authenticate
middleware_chain.purpose: Verifies the JWT from the Authorization header and attaches decoded user context to req.user.

middleware_chain.name: validateOrderBody
middleware_chain.purpose: Runs Zod schema validation on the request body against CreateOrderRequestSchema.
middleware_chain.on_failure: Return 422 Unprocessable Entity with field-level error detail

middleware_chain.name: rateLimitByUser
middleware_chain.purpose: Checks Redis sliding window counter for this user ID — max 5 orders per 24 hours.
middleware_chain.on_failure: Return 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header

error.handling: Caught by global error handler, mapped to 422

steps.notes: Pure in-memory validation — no I/O. Checks: at least one line item, positive quantities, supported shipping region.

steps.action: call inventory service to check stock availability for all line items

error.handling: Return 409 Conflict with per-item availability detail

steps.action: call payment service to create a Stripe PaymentIntent for the order total
persist order to database with AWAITING_PAYMENT status

error.handling: Return 500. Void the Stripe PaymentIntent asynchronously via background job.

steps.notes: Order record includes paymentIntentId, lineItems snapshot, userId, shippingAddress, and reviewFlag if total > $10,000.

steps.action: publish order.created event to Kafka via outbox
steps.notes: Fire-and-forget via transactional outbox — event is written to the outbox table in the same DB transaction as step 3.0.

transaction.scope: Order record + order.created outbox entry written atomically in the same DB transaction

idempotency.key: Not idempotent at the HTTP layer — duplicate submissions create duplicate orders. Rate limiting mitigates abuse.

label: Get Order
description: Fetches a single order by ID. Demonstrates a business-only operation — no routing block, showing that routing is optional at layer_logic.

business.summary: Returns the current state of an order to the authenticated requester. Only the order owner or an admin may view an order.
rules: A customer may only view their own orders — they cannot access orders belonging to other customers.
Admins may view any order regardless of ownership.
A request for a non-existent order ID is treated as a 404 — no information about other orders is revealed.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT
The orderId path parameter is a valid UUID
outcomes: The current order record is returned, including status, line items, and fulfilment information.

failure_modes.condition: Order not found or does not belong to the requesting user
failure_modes.response: 404 Not Found — no information is leaked about whether the order exists for another user.

label: Handle Payment Completed
description: Processes a payment.completed event from Kafka. Transitions the order to CONFIRMED and emits order.confirmed.

business.summary: When Stripe confirms a payment, this operation transitions the corresponding order from AWAITING_PAYMENT to CONFIRMED, notifies downstream fulfilment systems, and emits an order.confirmed event. Orders flagged for manual review are transitioned to PENDING_REVIEW instead of CONFIRMED.
rules: Only orders in AWAITING_PAYMENT status may be transitioned by this operation.
Orders flagged for manual review (total > $10,000) are transitioned to PENDING_REVIEW, not CONFIRMED.
Duplicate payment.completed events for the same order must be idempotent — the second invocation is a no-op.
The paymentIntentId in the event must match the paymentIntentId stored on the order.
preconditions: A payment.completed Kafka event has been received with a valid orderId and paymentIntentId
outcomes: Order is transitioned to CONFIRMED (or PENDING_REVIEW if flagged)
order.confirmed event is emitted to Kafka
Customer receives an order confirmation notification (triggered by downstream notification service)

failure_modes.condition: Order not found for the given orderId
failure_modes.response: Event is logged as an anomaly and acknowledged — no retry. Prevents infinite retry loops on stale events.

failure_modes.condition: Order is not in AWAITING_PAYMENT status
failure_modes.response: Event is acknowledged as a duplicate and discarded — idempotency guard.
Event is rejected and sent to the dead-letter queue for manual investigation.

steps.action: fetch order by orderId from database

error.handling: If null, log anomaly and acknowledge event — no retry

steps.action: validate idempotency — check order is in AWAITING_PAYMENT and paymentIntentId matches
steps.notes: On status mismatch: ack as duplicate. On intent mismatch: send to DLQ.

steps.action: determine target status based on review flag

error.handling: Do not acknowledge event — Kafka will redeliver.

steps.action: publish order.confirmed event to Kafka via outbox

transaction.scope: Order status update + order.confirmed outbox entry written atomically

idempotency.key: orderId — status check in step 2.0 ensures duplicate events are no-ops

label: Update Order Status
description: Admin-only operation to manually transition an order to a new status. Used for support escalations and fulfilment edge cases where the automated state machine cannot proceed.

business.summary: Allows an admin to force-transition an order to a target status. Validates that the requested transition is permitted from the current status before applying it.
rules: Only admins may call this operation — customers cannot manually update their own order status.
The target status must be a valid transition from the order's current status (e.g. AWAITING_PAYMENT → CANCELLED is allowed; DELIVERED → AWAITING_PAYMENT is not).
Transitioning to CANCELLED emits an order.cancelled event so downstream systems (inventory, payments, notifications) can react.
Status changes are audit-logged with the admin's user ID and a reason.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT
The orderId path parameter is a valid UUID
The request body contains a valid target status and a non-empty reason
outcomes: Order status is updated to the requested target status
Status change is recorded in the audit log
If transitioned to CANCELLED, an order.cancelled event is emitted

failure_modes.condition: Requested status transition is invalid from the current status
failure_modes.response: 409 Conflict — response body includes the current status and valid transitions.

label: Handle Payment Failed
description: Processes a payment.failed event from Kafka. Transitions the order to PAYMENT_FAILED and emits order.cancelled.

business.summary: When Stripe reports a payment failure, this operation transitions the corresponding order from AWAITING_PAYMENT to PAYMENT_FAILED and emits an order.cancelled event so downstream systems (inventory, notifications) can react accordingly.
rules: Only orders in AWAITING_PAYMENT status may be transitioned by this operation.
Duplicate payment.failed events for the same order must be idempotent.
The customer's rate limit counter is decremented — a failed payment should not count against their daily order quota.
preconditions: A payment.failed Kafka event has been received with a valid orderId, paymentIntentId, and failure reason
outcomes: order.cancelled event is emitted to Kafka
Customer's 24-hour order rate limit counter is decremented by 1

failure_modes.condition: Order not found or already in a terminal state
failure_modes.response: Event is acknowledged as a duplicate and discarded.

steps.action: fetch order by orderId and validate it is in AWAITING_PAYMENT status
steps.notes: If not found or not AWAITING_PAYMENT, acknowledge event as duplicate — idempotency guard

steps.action: update order status to PAYMENT_FAILED in database

error.handling: Do not acknowledge — Kafka will redeliver

steps.action: decrement user rate limit counter in Redis
steps.notes: Best-effort — if Redis is unavailable, do not fail the event processing. The counter will expire naturally.

steps.action: publish order.cancelled event to Kafka via outbox

transaction.scope: Order status update + order.cancelled outbox entry written atomically

idempotency.key: orderId — status check in step 1.0 ensures duplicate events are no-ops
